creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents lack sufficient context and memory of prior interactions, limiting their effectiveness despite individual capability. He uses the analogy of a talented engineer with amnesia to illustrate the problem and frames this as a core challenge needing resolution in agent development.

videos.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
videos.summary: Ishaan Sehgal argues that agent identity and continuity should be centered on durable session logs rather than models, tools, or orchestration frameworks. He draws an analogy to save files in video games to illustrate that the log, not the compute or framework, is the persistent entity that defines an agent across different runtimes and machines.

videos.title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
videos.summary: Raymond Weitekamp examines Recursive Language Models and how their inference-time compute patterns can be applied to coding agents. The approach aims to improve performance and reliability in agent behavior.

videos.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
videos.summary: Nishant Gupta from Meta Superintelligence Labs argues that traditional offline benchmarks and static datasets fail for autonomous AI systems in production. He presents architectural patterns for building continuous evaluation pipelines that measure tool use, planning, reasoning, drift detection, and operational reliability rather than model accuracy alone.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the AI model accounts for only 10% of coding outcomes while the surrounding harness of context, tools, guardrails, and verification comprises the other 90%. The video covers the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, the importance of context engineering, and the evolution of developer roles from conductor to orchestrator.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a memory layer that persists context across multiple AI agents and tools. It stores encrypted memories owned by the user that can be recalled by different AI providers like Claude and Pi without cross-provider visibility.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. According to Cole Medin, it demonstrates strong performance relative to its size and cost.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers increasingly use automated loops that prompt agents iteratively rather than prompting directly themselves, allowing agents to run unattended for extended periods. This approach compounds costs and hallucinations, requiring observability and control harnesses to manage reliability and model expenses.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason announced a new project involving Crabbox and Codebase harness tools available via GitHub. The project includes community resources through AI Builder Club and mentions Superdesign as a related product or tool in the ecosystem.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: Loop Engineer is a technique for setting up and building AI agents. The video provides practical guidance on implementation along with resources including a GitHub template, workshop materials, and code harness tools for developers getting started with agent development.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video tutorial on constructing proactive AI agents that improve autonomously through loops. Covers autonomous ads optimization and SEO as use cases, with tools like Loopany enabling memory and tool-based workflows for self-improving company systems.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature in autonomous coding systems and related tools. The video covers the /goal feature mechanics, setup instructions, prompt engineering for goals, and /Mission exploration for extended multi-step tasks. Tools discussed include Crewlet early access and Superdesign platform.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: An educational overview of how generative pre-trained transformers work, covering self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections. The video explains the fundamental mechanics that power leading models like Gemini, Opus, and Qwen.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code explains Andrej Karpathy's Bigram Language Model in 10 minutes, covering tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, and optimization. The video serves as a foundational piece for understanding larger language models like GPT, demonstrating core concepts like cross-entropy loss, softmax, and the AdamW optimizer.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series including Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture, MTP, and Latent Mixture of Experts to create distinctive technical features compared to other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a large language model that replaces full attention with MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA). The model includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache reading to reduce inference latency during prefill and decode stages.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Tool calling is the mechanism by which LLM-based agents request execution of external functions to access real-time data and take actions beyond model knowledge. The technique involves the model generating a tool request with parameters, a framework executing the tool, and looping to incorporate results. Tools consist of a schema, description, and handler function.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS is a tool that equips AI coding agents with up-to-date AWS documentation, best practices, and tested procedures. It reduces hallucination by providing verified knowledge and guardrails for working with AWS services, improving agent success rates and reducing wasted effort.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: Claude Code, an agentic coding tool by Anthropic, can now run on Amazon Bedrock with data remaining within AWS infrastructure. The video demonstrates deployment patterns including cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, per-team cost tracking, and Bedrock Guardrails for governance.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers explores how to stand out in tech job hunting when AI screens resumes, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and open-source contributions. The video addresses whether junior developers will be replaced, arguing that jobs evolve rather than disappear, and provides a 6-month plan for those without a computer science degree.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses code generation practices at Google compared to OpenAI and Anthropic. The video examines whether "vibing code" (collaborative or AI-assisted code generation) is actually practiced by Google engineers across different time periods from 2024 to 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses how Malus, a new tool or service, is impacting open source development and maintenance. The video examines whether Malus is fundamentally changing how open source projects operate and whether this constitutes a threat to the open source ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, Gong's co-founder, argues that software moats depend more on customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity than on code alone. He contends that AI-driven SaaS will not become obsolete and that the best AI products build devoted customer bases through superior product experience.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a ChatGPT-like breakthrough moment is coming for robotics, but warns against narrowly focusing on humanoid form factors. She advocates for designing robots with human interaction and delight in mind rather than pure utility, and suggests billions of robots will soon enter our lives.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against legacy enterprise software giants by building a full-featured platform from day one and leveraging trust from tier-one customers like Figma and Brex. She argues that climbing the technical and sales mountain early creates sustainable competitive advantage and that hiring discipline is critical to maintaining company standards.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that real product-market fit manifests as customer pull rather than push, warns against mistaking rabid fans for proof of PMF, and advises founders to embed in customer environments rather than conduct formal interviews. He emphasizes betting on AI model trajectories while avoiding self-serving delusions.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that businesses have overlooked critical security threats posed by agentic AI, including prompt injection, unauthorized data deletion, and unintended actions. He contends that these risks are causing business hesitation to adopt agentic systems despite their potential.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof announced a tech podcast focused on Anthropic with guest discussions. The short-form content emphasizes Anthropic as a notable AI company and covers the tech industry landscape.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: Small agile teams are presented as more productive than large teams, which are portrayed as bogged down in meetings. The piece argues that efficiency is superior in smaller units.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: The video discusses how AI demand is driving growth in high bandwidth memory, with Micron and SK Hynix positioned as key players shaping the memory stack for AI systems. The commentary predicts huge growth ahead for AI memory.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
